critique.summary: Kalpa is a Linux desktop distribution that offers an atomic and transactional system using the Plasma Desktop Environment from the KDE Project. It is based on openSUSE Tumbleweed and MicroOS, and is a member of the openSUSE Project.
mainPoints: Kalpa provides an atomic and transactional Linux desktop experience.
It uses the Plasma Desktop Environment from the KDE Project.
The desktop is derived from openSUSE Tumbleweed.
The base system is derived from openSUSE MicroOS.
Kalpa is a member of the openSUSE Project.
Installation media and documentation are available online.
Community engagement is facilitated through Matrix Chat, Mastodon, openSUSE Forums, and OpenBuildService.
critique.truthfulness: The claims about Kalpa being an atomic and transactional Linux desktop based on openSUSE Tumbleweed and MicroOS, and using the KDE Plasma Desktop Environment, are consistent with information available from the openSUSE community and related Linux distribution sources. The project appears to be a legitimate member of the openSUSE ecosystem. However, the article lacks citations or references to external sources to independently verify these claims.
considerations: The article does not provide detailed technical specifications or comparisons with other similar Linux distributions.
There is no information on the project's development status, release cycle, or user base size.
Security features and update mechanisms of the atomic and transactional system are not discussed.
No external sources or third-party reviews are cited to corroborate the project's claims.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers on the new OpenSUSE Kalpa project, with commenters expressing confusion about its purpose and advantages compared to existing atomic Linux distributions. Some users share personal experiences with similar distros, while others critique the website's lack of clarity and polish given its early development stage.

comments.author: stryan
comments.text: Kalpa is great and hits way above its alpha status; I've been running it on my laptop for months now with zero issues. It's been really nice to not have to worry about updates, just gotta reboot it every now and then and most things just work.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a firsthand user experience, which is valuable and likely accurate given the detailed usage report.

comments.author: quantummagic
comments.text:
This web page doesn't do a good job of motivating the reader.

I understand what the Plasma Desktop Environment is. But what is "atomic and transactional Linux"? What are the advantages to the alternatives? What other projects are similar? What is the motivation for this project in particular? Most importantly, why should I want to use it?
comments.analysis: Raises valid concerns about the lack of clear motivation and explanation on the project's website, which is a common issue for early-stage projects.

comments.author: sherr
comments.text:
This appears to be a "pre-beta" site, so this will be why it is not polished yet. From the documentation page :

"note: These installation instructions will be changing, with the Beta release of Kalpa"

A bit rough around the edges - so probably unfair to publicise too prominently yet.
comments.analysis: Provides context about the project's early development stage, explaining the roughness and lack of polish as expected for a pre-beta release.

comments.author: raphinou
comments.text: I've installed https://getaurora.dev/en/, another atomic Linux distro, for a non technical user and find it really good. I've read arguments that its architecture was better than kalpa, but I don't find it back and I have no sufficient knowledge or experience of both to have an opinion.
comments.analysis: Shares relevant experience with a similar atomic Linux distro, adding perspective though admits limited knowledge for direct comparison.

comments.text: This is a cool idea, but it’s not clear what problem it’s solving. Tumbleweed is already great
comments.analysis: Expresses skepticism about the project's value proposition, which is reasonable given the lack of clear problem statement.

comments.author: zokier
comments.text: This blog post might provide useful context: https://sfalken.tech/posts/2024-06-08-how-do-aeon-and-kalpa-...
comments.analysis: Offers an external resource for further understanding, which is helpful but depends on the linked content's quality.

comments.author: butILoveLife
comments.text:
Has anyone had a good long term experience with Atomic?

I admittedly only used it on a 13 year old gaming computer and couldn't get the GPU drivers because... you know containers.

This is something trivial with a regular install. (Especially with LLMs to assist)

I want to like Atomic, but it feels like an Apple-like regression in computing.
comments.analysis: Shares a personal negative experience with atomic Linux related to hardware compatibility, highlighting potential limitations.

comments.author: vishnuharidas
comments.text: A screenshot is the bare minimum for such things.
comments.analysis: A brief but reasonable suggestion that the project should provide visual aids to improve user understanding.

comments.author: KeyBoardG
comments.text: Are they still managing versions and rollback via BTRFS snapshots?
comments.analysis: Asks a technical question about the project's implementation details, showing engagement but no factual claim.

comments.author: giancarlostoro
comments.text: I wanted to try an Atomic Linux, I think I tried the Fedora flavor, nothing really worked for me for some reason, I gave in to Arch and tried it a la EndeavourOS. Have not looked back since.
comments.analysis: Shares personal experience with atomic Linux distros and preference for Arch-based systems, adding anecdotal context.

comments.author: dizhn
comments.text: Interesting they are hosting on codeberg. Opensuse has a pretty established hosting/build architecture provided by Suse.
comments.analysis: Observes the choice of hosting platform, which is factual and may imply project independence or other reasons.

comments.author: sach1
comments.text: This rules but the landing page could benefit from a Download Now type button for the iso page.
comments.analysis: Provides a simple usability suggestion to improve the website's user experience.

comments.author: shevy-java
comments.text: Isn't OpenSUSE for sale? At the least distrowatch said this recently.
comments.analysis: Makes an unsubstantiated claim about OpenSUSE's status that appears inaccurate and lacks evidence.

comments.author: TiredOfLife
comments.text: What's with the Ventoy hate. Every linux distro can be installed with Ventoy except for SuSe ones
comments.analysis: Expresses frustration about Ventoy compatibility issues with SuSe distros, which is a known limitation.
